topics.name: Identity & Culture — Me, My Family & Friends

questions.question: What is the German word for 'the family'?
questions.explanation: Die Familie means 'the family' in German. It is a feminine noun, so it uses the article 'die'.

questions.question: Which word means 'the parents'?
questions.explanation: Die Eltern is the German word for 'the parents'. It is always plural in form.

questions.question: What is 'the brother' in German?
questions.explanation: Der Bruder means 'the brother'. It is a masculine noun, so it uses the article 'der'.

questions.question: Which of these means 'the sister'?
questions.explanation: Die Schwester is the German word for 'the sister'. It is a feminine noun that uses the article 'die'.

questions.question: What does 'der Sohn' mean in English?
questions.explanation: Der Sohn translates to 'the son' in English. It is a masculine noun.

questions.question: Which word means 'the daughter'?
questions.explanation: Die Tochter means 'the daughter' in German. It is a feminine noun that uses the article 'die'.

questions.question: What is the German word for 'the husband'?
questions.explanation: Der Ehemann means 'the husband' in German. Der Mann can also mean 'the man' or 'the husband' in some contexts.

questions.question: Which word means 'the wife' or 'the woman'?
questions.explanation: Die Ehefrau means 'the wife' in German. Die Frau can mean both 'the woman' and 'the wife'.

questions.question: What does 'der Freund' mean?
questions.explanation: Der Freund can mean 'the boyfriend' or 'the friend' (masculine) in German. It is a masculine noun.

questions.question: Which verb means 'to get married'?
questions.explanation: Heiraten means 'to get married' in German. It is a regular verb that is commonly used when talking about relationships.

questions.question: Die Schwester means 'the brother'.
questions.explanation: Die Schwester actually means 'the sister'. Der Bruder means 'the brother'.

questions.question: Die Eltern is the German word for 'the parents'.
questions.explanation: Die Eltern is the correct German word for 'the parents'. This word is always used in plural form.

questions.question: Der Sohn means 'the daughter'.
questions.explanation: Der Sohn means 'the son'. Die Tochter means 'the daughter'.

questions.question: Die Freundin can mean 'the girlfriend'.
questions.explanation: Die Freundin means 'the girlfriend' or 'the friend' (feminine form). It uses the feminine article 'die'.

questions.question: Heiraten means 'to have' in German.
questions.explanation: Heiraten means 'to get married'. The verb 'to have' is 'haben' in German.

questions.question: Der Mann can mean 'the husband'.
questions.explanation: Der Mann can mean both 'the man' and 'the husband' in German. Der Ehemann is a more specific term for 'the husband'.

questions.question: Die Familie uses the masculine article 'der'.
questions.explanation: Die Familie is feminine and uses the feminine article 'die', not 'der'.

questions.question: Die Tochter and die Schwester mean the same thing.
questions.explanation: Die Tochter means 'the daughter' while die Schwester means 'the sister'. They are different family relationships.

questions.question: Der Bruder is a masculine noun.
questions.explanation: Der Bruder is masculine, which is why it uses the masculine article 'der'.

questions.question: Die Ehefrau means 'the girlfriend'.
questions.explanation: Die Ehefrau means 'the wife'. Die Freundin means 'the girlfriend' or 'the friend'.

questions.question: My mother is married to my father. They are ________.
questions.explanation: Die Eltern (the parents) is the correct answer because it refers to both the mother and father together.

questions.question: A young man who is in a romantic relationship is called ________.
questions.explanation: Der Freund can mean 'the boyfriend' when referring to a young man in a romantic relationship.

questions.question: My father and mother are getting married. They will ________.
questions.explanation: Heiraten means 'to get married'. This is the correct verb to use when talking about a wedding.

questions.question: I have a son and a daughter. My son is my ________ and my daughter is my ________.
questions.explanation: Der Sohn means 'the son' and die Tochter means 'the daughter'. These are the correct words for male and female children.

questions.question: My sibling who is female is my ________.
questions.explanation: Die Schwester means 'the sister'. This is the correct word for a female sibling.

questions.question: In German, the word for 'family' is ________.
questions.explanation: Familie is the German word for 'family'. The full phrase is 'die Familie' with the feminine article 'die'.

questions.question: My sibling who is male is my ________.
questions.explanation: Der Bruder means 'the brother'. This is the correct word for a male sibling.

questions.question: A woman in a romantic relationship with a man is called ________.
questions.explanation: Die Freundin means 'the girlfriend' when referring to a woman in a romantic relationship.

questions.question: The female person married to a man is called ________.
questions.explanation: Die Ehefrau is the specific German word for 'the wife'. Die Frau can also mean 'wife' in some contexts.

questions.question: The male person married to a woman is called ________ or ________.
questions.explanation: Both der Mann and der Ehemann can mean 'the husband'. Der Ehemann is the more specific term, while der Mann can also mean 'the man'.

questions.question: Match each term to its correct description.
What does 'nett' mean?
questions.explanation: Nett is a German adjective meaning nice or kind. It describes someone with a pleasant and caring nature.

questions.question: Which word means 'friendly'?
questions.explanation: Freundlich is the German word for friendly. It describes someone who is kind and pleasant to others.

questions.question: What is the German word for 'funny'?
questions.explanation: Lustig means funny or amusing in German. It describes someone who makes others laugh or entertains them.

questions.question: If someone is 'gesprächig', what are they like?
questions.explanation: Gesprächig means chatty or talkative. It describes someone who likes to talk a lot and enjoy conversations.

questions.question: What does 'schüchtern' mean?
questions.explanation: Schüchtern means shy or timid in German. It describes someone who is nervous or lacks confidence in social situations.

questions.question: Which word describes someone who doesn't want to work?
questions.explanation: Faul means lazy in German. It describes someone who doesn't want to work hard or make an effort.

questions.question: What is 'fleißig'?
questions.explanation: Fleißig means hardworking or diligent in German. It describes someone who works hard and puts in effort.

questions.question: If someone is 'egoistisch', they are:
questions.explanation: Egoistisch means selfish in German. It describes someone who only thinks about themselves and not about others.

questions.question: What does 'ehrlich' mean?
questions.explanation: Ehrlich means honest in German. It describes someone who tells the truth and is truthful.

questions.question: Which phrase means 'to argue' or 'to quarrel'?
questions.explanation: Sich streiten means to argue or quarrel in German. It describes when two people have a disagreement or fight verbally.

questions.question: Nett means lazy.
questions.explanation: Nett means nice or kind, not lazy. Faul is the word for lazy.

questions.question: Freundlich describes someone who is friendly.
questions.explanation: Freundlich is the correct German word for friendly, describing someone with a pleasant nature.

questions.question: Lustig means honest.
questions.explanation: Lustig means funny or amusing. Ehrlich is the word for honest.

questions.question: Someone who is gesprächig likes to talk a lot.
questions.explanation: Gesprächig means chatty or talkative, so yes, someone who is gesprächig likes to talk a lot.

questions.question: Schüchtern means outgoing and confident.
questions.explanation: Schüchtern means shy or timid, which is the opposite of outgoing and confident.

questions.question: Faul describes someone who works very hard.
questions.explanation: Faul means lazy. Fleißig is the word that describes someone who works hard.

questions.question: Fleißig is a positive personality trait.
questions.explanation: Fleißig means hardworking or diligent, which is a positive character trait.

questions.question: Egoistisch means thinking about other people first.
questions.explanation: Egoistisch means selfish, which means thinking about yourself first, not about other people.

questions.question: Ehrlich means someone tells the truth.
questions.explanation: Ehrlich means honest, so yes, someone who is ehrlich tells the truth.

questions.question: Sich streiten means to play together happily.
questions.explanation: Sich streiten means to argue or quarrel, which is the opposite of playing together happily.

questions.question: My friend is very ________ — she is always kind and nice to everyone.
questions.explanation: Nett means nice or kind, so it fits perfectly in this sentence about someone who is always kind to others.

questions.question: The new student is ________ and smiles at everyone.
questions.explanation: Freundlich means friendly, which describes someone who smiles and is pleasant to everyone.

questions.question: My brother is very ________ — he makes me laugh all the time.
questions.explanation: Lustig means funny or amusing, so it describes someone who makes others laugh.

questions.question: Sarah is very ________ at parties; she loves to talk with everyone.
questions.explanation: Gesprächig means chatty or talkative, which describes someone who likes to talk a lot at parties.

questions.question: Tom is very ________ in class and doesn't like to speak in front of others.
questions.explanation: Schüchtern means shy or timid, which describes someone who doesn't like to speak in front of others.

questions.question: My dad doesn't like to work — he is quite ________.
questions.explanation: Faul means lazy, so it describes someone who doesn't like to work.

questions.question: Emma always completes her homework on time. She is very ________.
questions.explanation: Fleißig means hardworking or diligent, which describes someone who always completes their work on time.

questions.question: He only thinks about himself and never helps others. He is ________.
questions.explanation: Egoistisch means selfish, which describes someone who only thinks about themselves.

questions.question: You can always trust what my sister says because she is very ________.
questions.explanation: Ehrlich means honest, so it describes someone you can trust because they tell the truth.

questions.question: My friends ________ about who gets to play first in the game.
questions.explanation: Sich streiten means to argue or quarrel, so 'streiten sich' (they argue) fits this sentence about friends disagreeing.

questions.question: Match each term to its correct description.
What does 'die Freundschaft' mean in English?
questions.explanation: Die Freundschaft is the German word for friendship, which is an important relationship to discuss in this topic.

questions.question: Which German word means 'to support'?
questions.explanation: Unterstützen means to support, an important action in relationships with friends and family.

questions.question: What is 'das Vertrauen'?
questions.explanation: Das Vertrauen means trust or confidence, which is essential in friendships and relationships.

questions.question: Which word means 'to share'?
questions.explanation: Teilen means to share, something people do regularly on social media and with friends.

questions.question: What does 'das soziale Netzwerk' refer to?
questions.explanation: Das soziale Netzwerk means social network, referring to platforms like Facebook and Instagram.

questions.question: What is 'das Handy'?
questions.explanation: Das Handy is the German word for a mobile phone, which many people use to access social media.

questions.question: Which verb means 'to download'?
questions.explanation: Herunterladen means to download, which you do when getting apps or files on your phone.

questions.question: What does 'online' mean?
questions.explanation: Online means connected to the internet, where you interact on social media.

questions.question: What is 'das Mobbing'?
questions.explanation: Das Mobbing means bullying or harassment, a serious issue that can damage relationships.

questions.question: What does 'das Cybermobbing' mean?
questions.explanation: Das Cybermobbing means cyberbullying, which is bullying that happens online through digital devices.

questions.question: Die Freundschaft means family.
questions.explanation: Die Freundschaft means friendship, not family. Family would be 'die Familie'.

questions.question: Das Vertrauen is an important part of good friendships.
questions.explanation: Trust and confidence are essential foundations for strong and healthy friendships.

questions.question: Teilen means to delete.
questions.explanation: Teilen means to share, not to delete. It's what you do with photos and messages on social media.

questions.question: You can use das Handy to access social networks.
questions.explanation: A mobile phone (das Handy) is one of the main devices people use to access social networks.

questions.question: Das Cybermobbing happens only in the real world, not online.
questions.explanation: Das Cybermobbing specifically refers to bullying that happens online through digital platforms.

questions.question: Unterstützen means to support someone.
questions.explanation: Unterstützen means to support, which is an important way to show you care in relationships.

questions.question: Das soziale Netzwerk refers to a group of friends at school.
questions.explanation: Das soziale Netzwerk refers to online social media platforms, not physical groups at school.

questions.question: Herunterladen means you are getting something from the internet onto your device.
questions.explanation: Herunterladen means to download, which is when you get files or apps from the internet.

questions.question: Das Mobbing is the same thing as das Cybermobbing.
questions.explanation: Das Mobbing is general bullying, while das Cybermobbing is specifically bullying that happens online.

questions.question: Many young people spend time online on social networks every day.
questions.explanation: Most young people today regularly use social networks and spend significant time online communicating with friends.

questions.question: __________ is very important in any friendship because you need to trust your friends.
questions.explanation: Trust (das Vertrauen) is a fundamental element of strong friendships and relationships.

questions.question: I use my __________ to message my friends and check social media.
questions.explanation: Most people use their mobile phone (Handy) to stay connected with friends online.

questions.question: Good friends __________ each other when they have problems.
questions.explanation: Friends support (unterstützen) each other through difficult times in their relationships.

questions.question: My best friend and I love to __________ photos and funny videos on Instagram.
questions.explanation: People share (teilen) content like photos and videos with their friends on social media.

questions.question: Facebook and Instagram are examples of __________ where young people connect with friends.
questions.explanation: Facebook and Instagram are social networks (soziale Netzwerke) that people use to stay in touch.

questions.question: I need to __________ this app on my phone so I can use it to talk to my friends.
questions.explanation: You download (herunterladen) apps onto your phone so you can use them.

questions.question: When you are __________, you are connected to the internet and can use social media.
questions.explanation: Being online means you are connected to the internet and can access digital platforms.

questions.question: __________ is a serious problem in schools where one student hurts another.
questions.explanation: Bullying (das Mobbing) is a serious issue that negatively affects victims and their well-being.

questions.question: __________ happens when someone sends mean messages or posts hurtful things about you online.
questions.explanation: Cyberbullying (das Cybermobbing) is bullying that occurs through digital platforms and can be very harmful.

questions.question: A true __________ is based on honesty, support, and trust between two people.
questions.explanation: Friendship (Freundschaft) is built on the foundation of trust, honesty, and mutual support.

questions.question: Match each term to its correct description.

options.match: a mobile phone used to access social media
bullying that happens online through digital platforms
to help or support someone you care about
an online platform where people connect and share content
confidence and belief in someone's honesty and reliability

questions.question: What is 'der Computer' in English?
questions.explanation: Der Computer is the German word for computer. It is one of the most basic pieces of technology vocabulary.

questions.question: Which German word means 'the tablet'?
questions.explanation: Das Tablet is the German word for tablet, a type of portable digital device.

questions.question: What does 'im Internet surfen' mean?
questions.explanation: Im Internet surfen literally translates to 'to surf the internet' and means to browse or use the internet.

questions.question: Which phrase means 'to send a message' in German?
questions.explanation: Eine Nachricht senden means to send a message. This is commonly used when talking about texting or messaging someone.

questions.question: What is 'das Videospiel'?
questions.explanation: Das Videospiel is the German word for video game, a form of entertainment played on digital devices.

questions.question: Which term refers to 'social media' in German?
questions.explanation: Die sozialen Medien is the German term for social media, platforms like Facebook, Instagram and TikTok.

questions.question: What does 'die App' mean in English?
questions.explanation: Die App is a shortened German word for application, referring to software programs on devices like phones and tablets.

questions.question: Which German word means 'the password'?
questions.explanation: Das Passwort is the German word for password, used to protect and secure online accounts.

questions.question: What is 'die Privatsphäre' in English?
questions.explanation: Die Privatsphäre means privacy, which is an important concept when discussing technology and social media.

questions.question: Which word means 'digital' in German?
questions.explanation: Digital is the same in both German and English and is used to describe technology and electronic devices.

questions.question: Das Tablet is a computer.
questions.explanation: A tablet is a type of digital device similar to a computer, so this statement is true.

questions.question: Das Passwort means 'the app'.
questions.explanation: Das Passwort means password, not app. Die App means the app.

questions.question: You can use die App on a smartphone.
questions.explanation: Apps are applications designed to run on smartphones and tablets, so this is true.

questions.question: Die sozialen Medien is the same as a video game.
questions.explanation: Die sozialen Medien (social media) and das Videospiel (video game) are different things.

questions.question: Im Internet surfen means to use the internet.
questions.explanation: Im Internet surfen literally means to surf the internet, which is used to describe browsing or using the internet.

questions.question: Eine Nachricht senden means to play a game.
questions.explanation: Eine Nachricht senden means to send a message, not to play a game.

questions.question: Die Privatsphäre is important when using social media.
questions.explanation: Privacy is indeed important when using social media to protect personal information.

questions.question: Der Computer is always portable.
questions.explanation: A computer can be a desktop (not portable) or a laptop (portable), so not all computers are portable.

questions.question: Digital technology includes smartphones and computers.
questions.explanation: Both smartphones and computers are examples of digital technology.

questions.question: Das Passwort should be shared with friends.
questions.explanation: Passwords should never be shared with others for security reasons.

questions.question: I like to play ________ on my tablet.
questions.explanation: Videospiele (video games) is the plural form and fits the blank when talking about games played on devices.

questions.question: I use my ________ to surf the internet every day.
questions.explanation: Computer is the device commonly used to surf the internet.

questions.question: I need to ________ senden to contact my friend.
questions.explanation: Eine Nachricht senden means to send a message, so 'eine Nachricht' completes the phrase.

questions.question: My ________ is a secret code I use to log in.
questions.explanation: A password (Passwort) is a secret code used to log into accounts and protect personal information.

questions.question: I download a new ________ for my phone every week.
questions.explanation: An app (App) is a small program or application downloaded onto phones and tablets.

questions.question: I spend too much time on ________ like Instagram and Facebook.
questions.explanation: Sozialen Medien (social media) refers to platforms like Instagram and Facebook.

questions.question: My ________ is important, so I don't share personal information online.
questions.explanation: Privacy (Privatsphäre) should be protected by not sharing personal information online.

questions.question: I prefer my ________ tablet to my old computer.
questions.explanation: Digital is used as an adjective to describe modern electronic devices and technology.

questions.question: My ________ is smaller and easier to carry than my computer.
questions.explanation: A tablet (Tablet) is a portable device that is smaller and lighter than most computers.

questions.question: I enjoy ________ surfen in my free time to relax.
questions.explanation: Im Internet surfen means to surf the internet, so 'im Internet' completes the phrase.

questions.question: Match each term to its correct description.

options.match: a secret code used to protect accounts
to browse or use the internet
personal information that should be kept private
a game played on a digital device

questions.question: What does 'die Freizeit' mean?
questions.explanation: Die Freizeit is the German word for free time or leisure time when you are not working or studying.

questions.question: Which activity is 'Fußball spielen'?
questions.explanation: Fußball spielen means to play football (or soccer in American English).

questions.question: What does 'schwimmen' mean?
questions.explanation: Schwimmen is the German verb meaning to swim.

questions.question: Which word means 'to read'?
questions.explanation: Lesen is the German verb meaning to read.

questions.question: What is 'Musik hören'?
questions.explanation: Musik hören means to listen to music. 'Hören' means to hear or listen.

questions.question: Which activity is 'fernsehen'?
questions.explanation: Fernsehen means to watch TV or television. 'Fern' means distant and 'sehen' means to see.

questions.question: What does 'einkaufen gehen' mean?
questions.explanation: Einkaufen gehen means to go shopping. 'Einkaufen' means shopping and 'gehen' means to go.

questions.question: Which phrase means 'to rest' or 'to relax'?
questions.explanation: Sich erholen is a reflexive verb meaning to rest or relax.

questions.question: What is 'das Spiel'?
questions.explanation: Das Spiel means the game or the match in German.

questions.question: What does 'die Mannschaft' mean?
questions.explanation: Die Mannschaft is the German word for the team.

questions.question: Die Freizeit means 'work time'.
questions.explanation: Die Freizeit means 'free time' or 'leisure time', not work time.

questions.question: Schwimmen is a hobby that involves water.
questions.explanation: Schwimmen (to swim) is indeed a water-based hobby.

questions.question: Lesen means 'to write'.
questions.explanation: Lesen means 'to read', not 'to write'. 'Schreiben' means to write.

questions.question: Fernsehen is the same as listening to the radio.
questions.explanation: Fernsehen means watching TV, which is different from listening to the radio.

questions.question: Einkaufen gehen is a leisure activity.
questions.explanation: Going shopping (einkaufen gehen) is a leisure activity that people do in their free time.

questions.question: Sich erholen means 'to play sports'.
questions.explanation: Sich erholen means 'to rest' or 'to relax', not 'to play sports'.

questions.question: Das Match and das Spiel both mean 'the game'.
questions.explanation: Both das Match and das Spiel can mean 'the game' or 'the match' in German.

questions.question: Die Mannschaft refers to a single sports player.
questions.explanation: Die Mannschaft means 'the team', not a single player.

questions.question: Musik hören involves listening rather than playing.
questions.explanation: Musik hören means 'to listen to music', which involves listening, not playing an instrument.

questions.question: Fußball spielen is the same as fernsehen.
questions.explanation: Fußball spielen means 'to play football' while fernsehen means 'to watch TV' - they are different activities.

questions.question: In my ________, I like to play football and listen to music.
questions.explanation: Freizeit (free time) is the correct word to complete this sentence about hobbies and leisure activities.

questions.question: Am Wochenende gehe ich ________ und kaufe neue Kleidung.
questions.explanation: Einkaufen (shopping) completes this sentence: 'At the weekend I go shopping and buy new clothes.'

questions.question: Meine Lieblingshobby ist ________, besonders im Sommer.
questions.explanation: Schwimmen (swimming) is a typical hobby, especially in summer when it's warm.

questions.question: Ich spiele ________ mit meinen Freunden jeden Tag nach der Schule.
questions.explanation: Fußball (football) completes this sentence about playing with friends after school.

questions.question: Am Abend ________ ich gerne und entspanne mich.
questions.explanation: Lese (read) completes this sentence: 'In the evening I like to read and relax.'

questions.question: Meine ________ ist sehr gut. Wir gewinnen viele Spiele!
questions.explanation: Mannschaft (team) completes this sentence about being part of a successful sports team.

questions.question: Ich höre gerne ________ und tanze dazu.
questions.explanation: Musik (music) completes this sentence: 'I like to listen to music and dance to it.'

questions.question: Am Samstagabend ________ wir fern und schauen einen Film.
questions.explanation: Sehen (watch/see) completes the verb 'fernsehen' (to watch TV) in this sentence.

questions.question: Das ________ zwischen unserer Schule und der anderen war sehr spannend!
questions.explanation: Spiel (game/match) completes this sentence about an exciting match between two schools.

questions.question: Nach dem Sport möchte ich mich ________ und ausruhen.
questions.explanation: Erholen (rest/relax) completes this sentence: 'After sport I want to rest and recover.'

questions.question: Match each term to its correct description.
What is the German word for a female singer?
questions.explanation: Die Sängerin is the feminine form of singer in German. Der Sänger would be a male singer.

questions.question: Which word means 'the band' in German?
questions.explanation: Die Band is the correct German translation for 'the band'. It's a common word used in music contexts.

questions.question: What type of film is ein Actionfilm?
questions.explanation: Ein Actionfilm literally translates to 'an action film'. This is a film genre with lots of fast-paced scenes and adventure.

questions.question: What is der Star in English?

questions.question: Which word means 'exciting' or 'gripping' in German?

questions.question: How would you say 'entertaining' in German?

questions.question: Spannend means 'boring'.

questions.question: Der Zeichentrickfilm is a type of animated or cartoon film.

questions.question: Unterhaltsam means 'entertaining'.
questions.explanation: Unterhaltsam does mean 'entertaining' and is used to describe something that keeps you interested and amused.

questions.question: Die Band and die Gruppe both mean 'the band' or 'the group' in German.
questions.explanation: Both words can be used to describe a musical band or group. They are synonyms in this context.

questions.question: Der Actionfilm is a type of comedy.
questions.explanation: Der Actionfilm is an action film, not a comedy. Die Komödie is the word for comedy films.

questions.question: My favourite band is very famous. They are a popular ________ with many fans around the world.
questions.explanation: Gruppe means 'group' and fits perfectly in the context of describing a band. Band would also be acceptable.

questions.question: I really enjoyed that film. The action was very ________ and kept me interested the whole time.
questions.explanation: Spannend means 'exciting' or 'gripping' and is the appropriate adjective to describe an interesting action film.

questions.question: I didn't like that TV ________ because it was not very interesting.
questions.explanation: Sendung means 'TV programme' or 'broadcast'. This word fits the context of television content.

questions.question: My little brother watched a new ________ on Saturday morning. It had colourful characters and funny stories.
questions.explanation: Zeichentrickfilm is a cartoon or animated film, which is appropriate for Saturday morning children's programming.

questions.question: The ________ of that band has an amazing voice and is very popular with fans.
questions.explanation: Sängerin is the female form of 'singer'. This completes the sentence about a female singer in a band.

questions.question: That film was really ________ . I fell asleep during the middle of it.

questions.question: Der Sport ist wichtig. __________, zum Beispiel, verbessert er die Gesundheit.
questions.explanation: Zum Beispiel (for example) introduces a specific example of why sport is important.

questions.question: __________ Ansicht nach sollten Schüler mehr Pausen haben.
questions.explanation: Meiner Ansicht nach (in my view) is the correct phrase; 'Meiner' is the first part of this opinion phrase.

questions.question: Match each term to its correct description.
